content.seo_title: For Freelancers: Antigravity Template to Solve Collaboration
content.seo_description: Master collaborative development as a freelancer. This advanced checklist uses the Antigravity project template and Claude Code to ship scalable SaaS apps fast.
content.answer_block: Collaboration bottlenecks kill freelance margins. By deploying the Antigravity project template, you standardize environment parity and architectural intent. Integrating this with Claude Code automates context-heavy tasks, allowing you to synchronize complex codebases across distributed teams instantly, slashing development friction and delivering production-grade SaaS features in record time.
content.intro:
You’re staring at a pull request that’s three days overdue, your client is asking for an update, and your local environment is out of sync with the staging branch. As a freelancer, your reputation is built on speed, but without a unified architectural foundation, collaboration becomes a chaotic game of telephone. The friction of 'it works on my machine' isn't just an annoyance; it’s a revenue leak.

The Antigravity project template is the antidote to this fragmentation. It enforces a rigorous, high-velocity structure that turns your codebase into a collaborative asset rather than a liability. When combined with the raw power of Claude Code, you don't just write code—you orchestrate complex systems that are ready for handoff, review, and rapid iteration from day one.

steps.title: Initialize the Antigravity Foundation
steps.description: Standardize your project structure to ensure all team members access the same architectural patterns.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Bootstrap a production-ready SaaS scaffold using the Antigravity template, enforcing strict TypeScript and modular architecture.

steps.title: Define Domain-Driven Context
steps.description: Inject business logic requirements into the project configuration to prevent scope creep during collaborative sessions.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Analyze the project requirements and generate a domain-driven file structure that isolates business logic from UI components.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run "refactor codebase to follow DDD principles defined in antigravity.config.json"

steps.title: Automate Environment Parity
steps.description: Use Claude Code to sync environment variables and dependency versions across all contributor machines.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate a robust .env.example and setup script that validates environment parity for all team members.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run "create a setup script that validates node version, dependencies, and environment keys"

steps.title: Implement Collaborative Guardrails
steps.description: Define automated linting and testing rules within the template to ensure code quality before it hits the repo.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Configure Husky hooks and ESLint rules that enforce the Antigravity standard for all incoming pull requests.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run "configure husky and lint-staged to enforce strict project standards"

steps.title: Deploy Continuous Documentation
steps.description: Automate the generation of technical documentation so collaborators understand the 'why' behind your code.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create an automated documentation pipeline that extracts JSDoc comments into a README-friendly format.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run "implement a script that updates architecture.md based on recent code changes"

pro_tips: Alias your most frequent Claude Code prompts to your shell profile to save 10+ minutes per feature branch.
Use Antigravity's 'ghost modules' to mock complex API dependencies during early-stage collaborative prototyping.
Always run a 'claude diff' before committing to ensure the AI hasn't introduced redundant boilerplate into your template.
Tag your architectural decisions with metadata in the Antigravity config to allow Claude to 'reason' about previous trade-offs.
Create a 'context.txt' file in your root directory; populate it with project constraints so Claude Code retains focus during long sessions.
content.next_steps: Once your template is live, invite your first collaborator, run a sync test using Claude Code, and begin building your MVP features with full architectural confidence.
content.conversion_block: Stop fighting your own codebase and start scaling your freelance output with professional-grade automation. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.
